heading: Military or naval discharge certificates
label: 498 Military or naval discharge certificates
text: Whoever forges, counterfeits, or falsely alters any certificate of discharge from the military or naval service of the United States, or uses, unlawfully possesses or exhibits any such certificate, knowing the same to be forged, counterfeited, or falsely altered, shall be fined under this title or imprisoned not more than one year, or both.1See 1994 Amendment note below.1
notes: Historical and Revision NotesBased on title 18, U.S.C., 1940, ed., § 136 (, ).Mar. 4, 1917, ch. 18039 Stat. 1182Reference to any person causing, procuring, aiding or assisting was omitted as unnecessary as such persons are made principals by .section 2 of this titleAt the end of this section words “in the discretion of the court” were omitted as unnecessary, as the punishment provisions, being framed in the alternative by the use of the disjunctive “or,” vest in the court the power to impose a fine or prison sentence in its discretion.Changes in phraseology were made.

Amendments1994—, which directed the amendment of this section by substituting “fined under this title” for “fined not more than $5,000”, was executed by making the substitution for “fined not more than $1,000”, to reflect the probable intent of Congress.Pub. L. 103–322
